{{Short description|none}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Barbados women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2023}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name                  = Barbados
| Badge                 = 
| Badge_size            = 200px
| Nickname              = ''Bajan Tridents''
| Association           = [[Barbados Football Association]] (BFA)
| Confederation         = [[CONCACAF]] (North America)
| Sub-confederation     = [[Caribbean Football Union|CFU]] (Caribbean)
| Coach                 = [[Kent Hall (footballer)|Kent Hall]]
| Captain               = [[Andre Applewhaite]]
| Most caps             = [[Norman Forde]] (74)
| Top scorer            = [[Llewellyn Riley]] (23)<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.soccer-db.info/index.php?option=com_joomsport&task=player&id=42605|title=Llewellyn Riley|website=www.soccer-db.info|access-date=20 August 2018|archive-date=20 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180820080040/http://www.soccer-db.info/index.php?option=com_joomsport&task=player&id=42605|url-status=live}}</ref>
| Home Stadium          = [[Wildey Turf]]
| FIFA Trigramme        = BRB
| FIFA Rank             = {{FIFA World Rankings|BRB}}
| FIFA max              = 92
| FIFA max date         = October 2009
| FIFA min              = 181
| FIFA min date         = July 2017
| Elo Rank              = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Barbados}}
| Elo max               = 106
| Elo max date          = 16 July 2000
| Elo min               = 181
| Elo min date          = November 2019
| pattern_la1           = _brb21h
| pattern_b1            = _brb21h
| pattern_ra1           = _brb21h
| pattern_sh1           = _brb21h
| pattern_so1           = 
| leftarm1              = FFD000
| body1                 = FFD000
| rightarm1             = FFD000
| shorts1               = FFD000
| socks1                = FFD000
| pattern_la2           = _brb21a
| pattern_b2            = _brb21a
| pattern_ra2           = _brb21a
| pattern_sh2           = 
| pattern_so2           = 
| leftarm2              = 000099
| body2                 = 000099
| rightarm2             = 000099
| shorts2               = 000099
| socks2                = 000099
| First game            = {{fb|BRB|1885}} 3–0 {{fb-rt|TRI|colonial}}<br>([[Bridgetown]], Barbados; 20 April 1929)
| Largest win           = {{nowrap|{{fb-rt|BRB}} 7–1 {{fb|AIA}}<br>([[St. John's, Antigua and Barbuda|St. John's]], Antigua and Barbuda; 24 September 2006)}}
| Largest loss          = {{fb|British Guiana}} 9–0 {{fb-rt|BRB|1885}}<br>([[Georgetown, Guyana|Georgetown]], British Guiana; 30 June 1931)<br>{{fb|TRI}} 9–0 {{fb-rt|BRB}}<br>([[Port of Spain]], Trinidad and Tobago; 25 March 2022)
| Regional name         = [[CFU Championship]] / [[Caribbean Cup]]
| Regional cup apps     = 9
| Regional cup first    = [[1978 CFU Championship|1978]]
| Regional cup best     = Runners-up ([[1985 CFU Championship|1985]])
| medaltemplates        = 
{{MedalCompetition|[[CFU Championship]]}}
{{MedalSilver|[[1985 CFU Championship|1985 Barbados]]|Team}}
}}

The '''Barbados national football team''' represents [[Barbados]] in men's international [[Association football|football]], which is governed by the [[Barbados Football Association]] founded in 1910. It has been a member of [[FIFA]] and [[CONCACAF]] since 1968. Regionally it is a member of [[Caribbean Football Union|CFU]] in the Caribbean Zone.

Barbados has never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup]] and the [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]], but has participated twice in League B and twice in League C of the [[CONCACAF Nations League]]. Regionally, the team finished as runners-up in the [[CFU Championship]] in [[1985 CFU Championship|1985]].

Barbados' debut in international competitions was in the [[1978 CFU Championship]]. Their first appearance in World Cup qualifiers was in the [[1977 CONCACAF Championship qualification]], which also served as the CONCACAF qualifiers for the [[1978 FIFA World Cup]]. The team achieved its first victory in 1929, defeating [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]] 3–0.

==History==
===Beginnings (1929–1980)===
Barbados played their first international match on April 20, 1929, against [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]],  ending in a 3–0 victory. In the 1930s, they participated in several editions of the Martínez Shield<ref>{{cite web |title=Martinez Shield |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesm/martinez.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=11 November 2021 |archive-date=14 April 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230414234729/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesm/martinez.html |url-status=live }}</ref> where they suffered their most severe defeat against [[Guyana national football team|Guyana]] (at that time, British Guiana), in [[Georgetown, Guyana|Georgetown]], in 1931, 9–0.

Barbados had to wait for the [[1972 CONCACAF Pre-Olympic Tournament qualification|qualifying tournament]] for the [[1972 Munich Olympic Games]] to play its first official competition match against the amateur team of [[El Salvador national football team|El Salvador]], on July 25, 1971, in [[Bridgetown]], a match that saw the Selecta win 0–3. Two years later, Barbados participated in the [[Football at the 1974 Central American and Caribbean Games|1974 Central American and Caribbean Games]], in [[Santo Domingo]], although it did not qualify past the first round. On August 15, 1976, Barbados defeated [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]] 2–1 (a brace by Victor Clarke) in the [[1977 CONCACAF Championship qualification|1978 World Cup qualifiers]], which doubled as qualification for the [[1977 CONCACAF Championship]]. In the second leg, the ''Soca Warriors'' claimed their revenge (1–0) in [[Port of Spain]], taking the tie to a third tiebreaker game, played in Bridgetown, on September 14, 1976, where Trinidad and Tobago won 1–3, eliminating Barbados.

===1980–2000===
In the '80s, the ''Bajan Braves'' obtained 2nd place in the [[1985 CFU Championship]]. The following year they played the [[Football at the 1986 Central American and Caribbean Games|1986 Central American and Caribbean Games]], where they reached the quarterfinals, before being eliminated by the hosts, [[Dominican Republic national football team|Dominican Republic]]. At the end of the decade, Barbados hosted the first edition of the [[1989 Caribbean Cup|Caribbean Cup]], without being able to progress beyond the group stage.

In the 90s, Barbados would return to the [[1994 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|World Cup qualifiers]], in the preliminary tournament for the [[1994 World Cup]], where it was again eliminated by [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]] (5–1). In the [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|1998 World Cup qualifiers]], after beating [[Dominica national football team|Dominica]] with an aggregate score of 2–0, Barbados was beaten down by [[Jamaica national football team|Jamaica]] (0–1 in Bridgetown and 2–0 in [[Kingston, Jamaica|Kingston]]). In the Caribbean Cup, the Bajan Braves qualified for the [[1994 Caribbean Cup|1994 competition]] in [[Barbados 4–2 Grenada|an infamous match]] against [[Grenada national football team|Grenada]] where they purposefully scored an own-goal in order to force overtime and help them to advance, and in the final tournament were eliminated in the first round. Barbados subsequently failed to qualify for the annual competition from 1995 to 1999.

===2000s===
The 21st century started well for Barbados, who eliminated [[Cuba national football team|Cuba]] on penalties (5–4), after two 1–1 draws in [[Havana]] and Bridgetown, during the [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|qualifying rounds]] for the [[2002 World Cup]]. In the second round, they achieved a historic victory at home against [[Costa Rica national football team|Costa Rica]] (2–1), on July 16, 2000, with goals from [[Llewellyn Riley]] and [[Michael Forde]]. However, after winning that match, the Barbadians lost the remaining fixtures, wasting their chance to advance to the final hexagonal.

They would return in 2001 to the final phase of the Caribbean Cup, although without much luck after being eliminated again in the first round. In the [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2006 World Cup qualifiers]], the [[Saint Kitts and Nevis national football team|Saint Kitts and Nevis]] team eliminated Barbados in the first phase, with an aggregate score of 5–2. In 2005, Barbados hosted (for the second time) the XII edition of the [[2005 Caribbean Cup|Caribbean Cup]], finishing in 4th place. They also qualified for the 2007 and 2008 tournaments, eliminated both times in the group stage. In the preliminary tournament for the [[2010 World Cup]], Barbados was eliminated by the [[United States men's national soccer team|United States]], over two legs, with a crushing aggregate result of 9–0.

===2010–present===
The qualifiers heading to the [[2014 World Cup]] were a real ordeal for the ''Bajan Braves'' who were placed into a group with [[Bermuda national football team|Bermuda]], [[Guyana national football team|Guyana]] and [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]]. They finished last in the group, with 2 goals scored and 14 conceded. After being eliminated in the preliminary phase of the Caribbean Cup in 2012 and after 17 months of inactivity, Barbados returned to play an international match, on March 2, 2014, against Jamaica, in Bridgetown, a match that concluded with victory for the ''Reggae Boyz'', 2–0.

In the qualifying rounds for the [[2018 World Cup]], they would face the [[United States Virgin Islands national football team|US Virgin Islands]] where they were surprised 0–1 at home, however, Barbados would overcome the deficit by a 0–4 victory on the road. Then they would face [[Aruba national football team|Aruba]], winning 0–2 as a visitor, then beating them 1–0 at home however Barbados had fielded an ineligible player, [[Hadan Holligan]], who was due to serve a suspension for collecting two yellow cards, therefore the second leg was awarded to Aruba 3–0, seeing them progress, eliminating Barbados.<ref>{{cite web |title=FIFA ousts Barbados from World Cup for ineligible player |url=https://apnews.com/article/5b085844711e4fe786c2985322a2e674 |website=AP News |date=29 June 2015 |access-date=11 November 2021 |archive-date=24 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220124045402/https://apnews.com/article/5b085844711e4fe786c2985322a2e674 |url-status=live }}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==
{{Main|Barbados national football team results (2020–present)}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF second round#Group C|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 4 June
|time       =
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BRB}}
|score      = 1–1
|team2      = {{fb|ARU}}
|goals1     =
*[[Omani Leacock|Leacock]] {{goal|7|pen.}}
|goals2     =
*{{goal|15}} [[Diederick Luydens|Luydens]] 
|location   = [[Wildey, Barbados|Wildey]], Barbados
|stadium    = [[Wildey Turf]]
|attendance =
|referee    = Andrew Samuel ([[Trinidad and Tobago Football Association|Trinidad and Tobago]])
|report     = [https://www.fifa.com/fifaplus/en/match-centre/match/520/288301/288302/400017744 Report (FIFA)]
|result     = D
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF second round#Group C|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 10 June
|time       =
|team1      = {{fb-rt|LCA}}
|score      = 2–1
|team2      = {{fb|BRB}}
|goals1     =
*[[Caniggia Elva|Elva]] {{goal|42|pen.|90|pen.}}
|goals2     =
*{{goal|12}} [[Devonte Richards|Richards]] 
|location   = [[Gros Islet]], Saint Lucia
|stadium    = [[Daren Sammy Cricket Ground]]
|attendance = 144
|referee    = Sergio Reyna ([[National Football Federation of Guatemala|Guatemala]])
|report     = [https://www.fifa.com/fifaplus/en/match-centre/match/520/288301/288302/400017749 Report (FIFA)]
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]]
|date       = 12 November
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BRB}}
|score      = 3–2
|team2      = {{fb|BON}}
|goals1     =
*[[Jaron Oughterson|Oughterson]] {{goal|10||45+1}}
*[[Ackeel Applewhaite|Applewhaite]] {{goal|34}}
|goals2     =
*{{goal|55}} [[Ayrton Cicilia|Cicilia]]
*{{goal|90}} [[Ninho Gerardo-Felicia|Gerardo-Felicia]]
|location   = [[North Sound]], [[Antigua and Barbuda]]
|stadium    = [[Sir Vivian Richards Stadium]] 
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|report     = https://www.11v11.com/matches/barbados-v-bonaire-12-november-2025-391592/
|result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]]
|date       = 15 November
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BRB}}
|score      = 0–3
|team2      = {{fb|ARU}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
*{{goal|4}} [[Quinlan Poulina|Poulina]]
*{{goal|59}} [[Jaybrien Romano|Romano]]
*{{goal|76}} [[Carlito Fermina|Fermina]]
|location   = [[North Sound]], [[Antigua and Barbuda]]
|stadium    = [[Sir Vivian Richards Stadium]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = Evens Julmis ([[Bahamas Football Association|Bahamas]])
|report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2025-11-15/barbados-vs-aruba/3936744
|result     = L
}}

===2026===
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]]
|date       = 26 March
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MAF}}
|score      = 3–1
|team2      = {{fb|BRB}}
|report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-26/saint-martin-vs-barbados/4095371
|goals1     = *[[Jean-Baptiste Léo|Léo]] {{goal|56||57}}
*[[Mario Williams (footballer)|Williams]] {{goal|87|o.g.}}
|goals2     = [[Jaron Oughterson|Oughterson]] {{goal|85}}
|location   = [[Rincon, Bonaire|Rincon]], Bonaire
|stadium    = [[Stadion Antonio Trenidat]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = [[Shekiel Jokil]] ([[Surinamese Football Association|Suriname]])
|result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]]
|date       = 29 March
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BRB}}
|score      = 2–3
|team2      = {{fb|SVG|wrap=yes}}
|report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-29/barbados-vs-st-vincent-and-the-grenadines/4126783
|goals1     = *[[Ajani Banton|Banton]] {{goal|51}}
*[[Jaron Oughterson|Oughterson]] {{goal|59}}
|goals2     = *[[Kirtney Franklyn|Franklyn]] {{goal|28}}
*[[Azinho Solomon|Solomon]] {{goal|38}}
*[[Oalex Anderson|Anderson]] {{goal|78}}
|location   = [[Rincon, Bonaire|Rincon]], Bonaire
|stadium    = [[Stadion Antonio Trenidat]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = [[Evens Julmis]] ([[Bahamas Football Association|Bahamas]])
|result     = L
}}

==Coaching history==

[[File:Emmerson Boyce - Wigan Athletic v Birmingham City, 5th December 2009.jpg|thumb|100px|[[Emmerson Boyce]] played for Barbados and managed Barbados]]

{{divcol|colwidth=22em}}
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Daniel Reid (Barbadian footballer)|Daniel Reid]]
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Tom Burke (Barbadian footballer)|Tom Burke]]
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[George Braathen]]
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Ethan Dallas]]
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Jordan Emmett]]
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Ryan Kontoh]]
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Kevin Millard]] (1992)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Keith Griffith]] (1994)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Edward Smith (footballer)|Edward Smith]] (1996)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Eyre Sealy]] (1998)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Horace Beckles]] (2000)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Sherlock Yarde]] (2001)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Keith Griffith]] (2002)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Allison John]] (2003)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Kenville Layne]] (2003–2004)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Mark Doherty]] (2005)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Eyre Sealy]] (2007–2008)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Keith Griffith]] (2008)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Thomas Jordan (footballer)|Thomas Jordan]] (2008–2010)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Colin Forde (footballer, born 1963)|Colin Forde]] (2011–2014)
* {{flagicon|BRA}} [[Marcos Falopa]] (2014–2015)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Colin Harewood]] (2015–2017)
* {{flagicon|SOM}} [[Ahmed Mohamed Ahmed]] (2017–2019)
* {{flagicon|TRI}} [[Russell Latapy]] (2019–2022)
* {{flagicon|POR}} [[Orlando da Costa (football manager)|Orlando da Costa]] (2022–2023)
* {{flagicon|BRB}} [[Emmerson Boyce]] (2023–2024)
*{{flagicon|BRB}} [[Kent Hall (footballer)|Kent Hall]] (2024–Present)<ref>{{cite web |last1=Desk |first1=Sports |title=Former Barbados midfielder Kent Hall takes over as head coach of Barbados senior men's national team |url=https://www.sportsmax.tv/football/regional/item/146661-former-barbados-midfielder-kent-hall-takes-over-as-head-coach-of-barbados-senior-men-s-national-team |website=Sportsmax |access-date=8 September 2024}}</ref>
{{div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]] matches against [[Saint Martin national football team|Saint Martin]] and [[Saint Vincent and the Grenadines national football team|Saint Vincent and the Grenadines]] on 26 and 29 March 2026.<ref>{{Cite Instagram|user= barbadosfa|
title=The Barbados Football Association confirms the upcoming fixtures for the 2025/26 Concacaf Friendly Series.|postid =DWQH4dRjmVb|date=March 23, 2026}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 30 March 2026, after the match against [[Saint Vincent and the Grenadines national football team|Saint Vincent and the Grenadines]]''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Jireh Malcolm]]|age={{bda|2006|11|21|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[Paradise FC (Barbados)|Paradise]]|clubnat=BRB}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Shaquan Phillips]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|02|04|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Weymouth Wales FC|Weymouth]]|clubnat=BRB}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Ackeel Applewhaite]]|age={{bda|1999|7|17|df=y}}|caps=43|goals=1|club=[[CT United FC|CT United]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Andre Applewhaite]]|age={{bda|2002|6|3|df=y}}|caps=26|goals=3|club=[[CT United FC|CT United]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Carl Hinkson]]|age={{bda|1997|4|14|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=1|club=[[Kick Start FC|Kick Start Rush]]|clubnat=BRB}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Ethan Taylor]]|age={{bda|2005|1|19|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=1|club=[[Appalachian FC]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Jaheim Neblett]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|09|30|df=yes}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Weymouth Wales FC|Weymouth Wales]]|clubnat=BRB}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Ajani Banton]]|age={{bda|2006|12|18|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=1|club=[[Lehigh Mountain Hawks]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Mario Williams (footballer)|Mario Williams]]|age={{bda|1992|8|19|df=y}}|caps=52|goals=0|club=[[Weymouth Wales FC|Weymouth Wales]]|clubnat=BRB}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Rashad Jules]]|age={{Bda|df=yes|1992|6|24}}|caps=35|goals=5|club=[[Weymouth Wales FC|Weymouth Wales]]|clubnat=BRB}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nadre Butcher]]|age={{Bda|2004|3|6|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=1|club=[[Real Sport Clube]]|clubnat=POR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nicoli Brathwaite]]|age={{bda|2000|12|24|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=0|club=[[ACS Progresul Ezeriș|Progresul Ezeriș]]|clubnat=ROU}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Jaron Oughterson]]|age={{bda|2001|5|11|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=1|club=[[UWI Blackbirds|UWI]]|clubnat=BRB}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Leon Elliott]]|age={{bda|2006|10|22|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Ipswich Town F.C. Under-21s and Academy|Ipswich Town U21]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Sheran Hoyte]]|age={{bda|2000|2|21|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=2|club=[[Central F.C.|Central FC]]|clubnat=TTO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Shay Prescod]]|age={{bda|2004|3|4|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Oklahoma City Stars]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up within the past 12 months.
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Justin Griffith (footballer)|Justin Griffith]]|age={{bda|2005|2|18|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Macclesfield F.C.|Macclesfield]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Ricardio Morris]]|age={{bda|1993|4|24|df=y}}|caps=50|goals=0|club=[[Weymouth Wales FC|Weymouth Wales]]|clubnat=BRB|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Jayden Goodridge]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|11|17|df=yes}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Pro-Shottas Soccer School]]|clubnat=BRB|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Romario Small]]|age={{birth date and age|1994|12|16|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Central F.C.|Central FC]]|clubnat=TTO|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Lemar Catlyn]]|age={{bda|2002|12|06|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Ellerton FC|Ellerton]]|clubnat=BRB|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Tyrique Bailey]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|04|21|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Union University Bulldogs]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Kamari Johnson]]|age={{birth date and age|2007|06|21|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Kick Start FC|Kick Start]]|clubnat=BRB|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Niall Reid-Stephen]]|age={{Bda|2001|9|8|df=y}}|caps=20|goals=11|club=[[New Mexico United]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Tajio James]]|age={{bda|2003|12|17|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=3|club=[[NK Radnik Križevci|Radnik Križevci]]|clubnat=CRO|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Jaylan Gilkes]]|age={{bda|2002|6|28|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Barbados Soccer Academy]]|clubnat=BRB|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Shamari Harewood]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|05|25|df=yes}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[UWI Blackbirds|UWI]]|clubnat=BRB|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Sekani Mayers]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|09|23|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Kick Start FC|Kick Start]]|clubnat=BRB|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Omani Leacock]]|age={{bda|df=yes|1998|5|1}}|caps=29|goals=4|club=[[Waterhouse F.C.|Waterhouse]]|clubnat=JAM|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Devonte Richards]]|age={{bda|2002|9|27|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=1|club=[[Central Arkansas Bears]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Khimani Cox]]|age={{Bda|2005|2|14|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Barbados Soccer Academy]]|clubnat=BRB|latest=v. {{fb|LCA}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|30 March 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Barbados |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/18/Barbados.html |website=National Football Teams |access-date=28 June 2023 |archive-date=2 July 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230702175021/https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/18/2023/Barbados.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Barbados.''

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Norman Forde]]
|72
|18
|1998–2011
|-
|2
|style="text-align: left;"|[[John Parris (footballer)|John Parris]]
|61
|4
|2000–2011
|-
|3
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Gregory Goodridge]]
|59
|16
|1995–2008
|-
|4
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Hadan Holligan]]
|55
|6
|2015–2024
|-
|5
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Mario Williams (Barbados footballer)|Mario Williams]]'''
|52
|0
|2015–present
|-
|6
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Mario Harte]]
|51
|13
|2008–2019
|-
|7
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Ricardio Morris]]'''
|50
|0
|2012–present
|-
|rowspan=2|8
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Ackeel Applewhaite]]'''
|43
|1
|2017–present
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Raheim Sargeant]]
|43
|3
|2010–2019
|-
|10
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Jonathan Straker]]
|42
|1
|1998–2011
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Llewellyn Riley]]
|22
|41
|{{#expr:22/41 round 2}}
|1995–2005
|-
|2
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Norman Forde]]
|18
|72
|{{#expr:18/72 round 2}}
|1998–2011
|-
|3
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Gregory Goodridge]]
|16
|61
|{{#expr:16/61 round 2}}
|1995–2008
|-
|4
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Mario Harte]]
|13
|51
|{{#expr:13/51 round 2}}
|2008–2019
|-
|rowspan=2|5
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Niall Reid-Stephen]]'''
|11
|20
|{{#expr:11/20 round 2}}
|2018–present
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Jeff Williams (footballer)|Jeff Williams]]
|11
|36
|{{#expr:11/36 round 2}}
|2005–2014
|-
|7
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Jerry Alexander]]
|9
|11
|{{#expr:9/11 round 2}}
|1995–2000
|-
|rowspan=2|8
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Thierry Gale]]'''
|7
|14
|{{#expr:7/14 round 2}}
|2018–present
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Riviere Williams]]
|7
|26
|{{#expr:7/26 round 2}}
|2003–2011
|-
|10
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Paul Ifill]]
|6
|10
|{{#expr:6/10 round 2}}
|2004–2008
|-
|}

==Competitive record==
===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!colspan=10|[[FIFA World Cup]] record 
!rowspan=29|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record 
|-
!Year
!Round
!{{Tooltip|Pos.|Position}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]]||colspan=9|''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6|''Not a FIFA member'' 
|-
|[[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]] and [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]||colspan=9|''Did not participate''
|colspan=6|''Did not participate'' 
|-
|{{flagicon|Argentina}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]||colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|3||1||0||2||3||5
|-
|{{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]||colspan=9|''Did not participate''
|colspan=6|''Did not participate'' 
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]||colspan=9|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]||colspan=9|''Did not participate''
|colspan=6|''Did not participate'' 
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]||colspan=9 rowspan=6|''Did not qualify''
|2||0||0||2||1||5
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|4||2||0||2||2||3
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|12||4||3||5||17||27
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|2||0||0||2||2||5
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|4||1||1||2||2||10
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|6||0||0||6||2||14
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]||colspan=9|''Disqualified after qualifying for third round''{{refn|FIFA awarded Aruba a 3–0 win as a result of Barbados fielding the ineligible player [[Hadan Holligan]]. Holligan failed to serve an automatic one match suspension for receiving two yellow cards earlier in the competition. The match originally ended 1–0 to Barbados}}<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/governance/news/y=2015/m=6/news=barbados-sanctioned-for-fielding-ineligible-player-2654185.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150703033641/http://www.fifa.com/governance/news/y=2015/m=6/news=barbados-sanctioned-for-fielding-ineligible-player-2654185.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=3 July 2015|title=Barbados sanctioned for fielding ineligible player|publisher=FIFA|date=29 June 2015}}</ref>
|4||2||0||2||6||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]||colspan=9 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|4||1||2||1||3||3
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|4||0||1||3||4||10
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]||colspan=9 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total!!—!!0/10!!colspan=7|—
!45!!11!!7!!27!!42!!86
|}

===CONCACAF Gold Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!colspan=10|[[CONCACAF Championship]] / [[CONCACAF Gold Cup|Gold Cup]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!{{Tooltip|Pos.|Position}}
!Pld
!W
!D
!L
!GF
!GA
!Squad
|-
|[[1963 CONCACAF Championship|1963]] to [[1973 CONCACAF Championship|1973]]||colspan=9|''Did not participate''
|-
|{{flagicon|MEX}} [[1977 CONCACAF Championship|1977]]||colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Honduras|1949}} [[1981 CONCACAF Championship|1981]]||colspan=9|''Withdrew''
|-
|[[1985 CONCACAF Championship|1985]] to [[1991 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1991]]||colspan=9|''Did not participate''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}}{{flagicon|USA}} [[1993 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1993]]||colspan=9 rowspan=17|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[1996 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1996]]
|-
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[1998 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1998]]
|-
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2000 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2000]]
|-
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2002 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2002]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}}{{flagicon|USA}} [[2003 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2003]]
|-
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2005 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2005]]
|-
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2007 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2007]]
|-
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2009 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2009]]
|-
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2011]]
|-
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2013 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2013]]
|-
|{{flagicon|CAN}}{{flagicon|USA}} [[2015 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2015]]
|-
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2017 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2017]]
|-
|{{flagicon|CRC}}{{flagicon|JAM}}{{flagicon|USA}} [[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2019]]
|-
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2021 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2021]]
|-
|{{flagicon|CAN}}{{flagicon|USA}} [[2023 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2023]]
|-
|{{flagicon|CAN}}{{flagicon|USA}} [[2025 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2025]]
|-
!Total!!—!!0/17!!colspan=7|—
|}

===CONCACAF Nations League===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!colspan=22|[[CONCACAF Nations League]] record
|-
!colspan=11|League phase
!rowspan=8|
!colspan=10|Final phase
|-
!Season
!{{Tooltip|Div.|Division}}
!Group
!{{Tooltip|Pos.|Position}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|P/R|Promotion/Relegation}}
!Finals
!Round
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Squad|Finals Squad Only}}
|-
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League|2019–20]]
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League C|C]]
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League C#Group A|A]]
|3rd||6||4||0||2||14||4||{{rise}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2021 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2021]]||colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Ineligible''
|-
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League|2022–23]]
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group A|A]]
|13th||6||1||0||5||3||9||{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2023 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2023]]
|-
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League|2023–24]]
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group B|B]]
|16th||6||0||0||6||7||26||{{fall}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2024 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2024]]
|-
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League|2024–25]]
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League C|C]]
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League C#Group A|A]]
|1st||4||4||0||0||17||4||{{rise}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2025 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2025]]
|-
|[[2026–27 CONCACAF Nations League|2026–27]]
|[[2026–27 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]||colspan=9|''To be determined''
|[[2027 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2027]]
|-
!colspan=4|Total!!22!!9
!!0!!13!!41!!43!!—
!Total!!—!!0/4!!colspan=6|—
|}
{| class="wikitable collapsible collapsed" style="text-align:center; font-size:90%;"
|-
!colspan=2|CONCACAF Nations League history
|-
!First match
|{{fb-rt|BRB}} 4–0 {{fb|MAF}}<br>(5 September 2019; [[Bridgetown]], Barbados)
|-
!Biggest win
|{{fb|VIR}} 0–5 {{fb-rt|BRB}}<br>(9 October 2024; [[Wildey, Barbados|Wildey]], Barbados)
|-
!Biggest defeat
|{{fb|DOM}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|BRB}}<br>(11 September 2023; [[Wildey, Barbados|Wildey]], Barbados)
|-
!Best result
|13th – League B ([[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League#League B|2022–23]])
|-
!Worst result
|Relegation League C ([[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League#League B|2023–24]])
|}

===Caribbean Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[CFU Championship]] / [[Caribbean Cup]] record
!rowspan=28|
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!{{Tooltip|Pos.|Position}}
!Pld
!W
!D
!L
!GF
!GA
!Pld
!W
!D
!L
!GF
!GA
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1978 CFU Championship|1978]]
|First round||—||2||0||1||1||0||1
|colspan=6|—
|-
|{{flagicon|SUR}} [[1979 CFU Championship|1979]]||colspan=8|''Did not participate''
|colspan=7|''Did not participate''
|-
|{{flagicon|Puerto Rico}} [[1981 CFU Championship|1981]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
||2||0||2||0||2||2
|-
|{{flagicon|French Guiana}} [[1983 CFU Championship|1983]]
||4||1||2||1||2||3
|-bgcolor=silver
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|Barbados}} [[1985 CFU Championship|1985]]||'''Runners-up'''||'''2nd'''||'''3'''||'''0'''||'''3'''||'''0'''||'''2'''||'''2'''
|colspan=6|''Qualified as host''
|-
|{{flagicon|Martinique|snake}} [[1988 CFU Championship|1988]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
||2||0||0||2||1||9
|- 
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|BAR}} [[1989 Caribbean Cup|1989]]
|Group stage||5th||2||1||0||1||1||3
|colspan=7|''Qualified as host''
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1990 Caribbean Cup|1990]] 
|''Unfinished''<ref>Play was suspended when [[Jamaat al Muslimeen]] [[Jamaat al Muslimeen coup attempt|attempted a ''coup d'état'']] of the [[Politics of Trinidad and Tobago|government of Trinidad and Tobago]]. The tournament was abandoned altogether after [[1990 Atlantic hurricane season#Tropical Storm Arthur|Tropical storm Arthur]] forced the cancellation of the final round of games. Trinidad and Tobago were to meet Martinique in the final.</ref>||— ||2||1||1||0||5||4
||3||2||1||0||3||1
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[1991 Caribbean Cup|1991]]||colspan=8|''Did not participate''
|colspan=6|''Did not participate''
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1992 Caribbean Cup|1992]]||Colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
||3||1||1||1||4||4
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[1993 Caribbean Cup|1993]]
||3||2||0||1||9||2
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1994 Caribbean Cup|1994]]
|Group stage||6th||3||0||2||1||3||5 
||2||1||0||1||4||3
|- 
|{{flagicon|CAY}} {{flagicon|JAM}} [[1995 Caribbean Cup|1995]]||colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not qualify''
||4||2||1||1||9||3
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1996 Caribbean Cup|1996]]
||2||1||0||1||2||2
|-
|{{flagicon|ATG}} {{flagicon|SKN}} [[1997 Caribbean Cup|1997]]
||2||1||1||0||3||1
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} {{flagicon|TRI}} [[1998 Caribbean Cup|1998]]
||3||1||0||2||6||9
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1999 Caribbean Cup|1999]]
||5||3||1||1||9||4
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[2001 Caribbean Cup|2001]]||Group stage||8th||3||0||0||3||2||10
||3||2||1||0||9||5
|-bgcolor=#9acdff
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|BRB}} [[2005 Caribbean Cup|2005]]
|'''Fourth place'''||'''4th'''||'''3'''||'''0'''||'''0'''||'''3'''||'''2'''||'''7'''
|colspan=7|''Qualified as host''
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[2007 Caribbean Cup|2007]]
|Group stage||8th||3||0||1||2||3||6
||6||4||2||0||17||5
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[2008 Caribbean Cup|2008]]
|Group stage||8th||3||0||0||3||4||8
||5||4||1||0||11||6
|-
|{{flagicon|MTQ|snake}} [[2010 Caribbean Cup|2010]]||colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Did not qualify''
||3||1||2||0||6||1
|-
|{{flagicon|ATG}} [[2012 Caribbean Cup|2012]]
||3||2||0||1||3||2
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[2014 Caribbean Cup|2014]]
||6||2||1||3||12||13
|-
|{{flagicon|MTQ|snake}} [[2017 Caribbean Cup|2017]]
||2||1||0||1||1||2
|-
!Total!!Runners-up!!9/21!!24!!2!!8!!14!!22!!46
!63!!31!!16!!16!!113!!77
|}

==Honours==
===Subregional===
* '''[[CFU Championship]]'''
** {{silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[1985 CFU Championship|1985]]

==Head-to-head record==
''As of 15 November 2025, These all-time records are exclusively class 'A' internationals matches''.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.11v11.com/teams/barbados/tab/stats|title=Barbados national football team statistics and records: All-time record|publisher=11v11.com|access-date=25 May 2020|archive-date=29 September 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210929230914/https://www.11v11.com/teams/barbados/tab/stats/|url-status=live}}</ref>

;Key
{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Winning record|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Equal record|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Losing record|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

{| class="sortable wikitable" style="text-align:center; font-size:100%;"
|-
! Opponent
! Pld 
! W
! D
! L
! GF
! GA
|- align="center"
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|AIA}} || 2 || 2 || 0 || 0 || 8 || 1
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|ATG}} || 9 || 6 || 1 || 2 || 16 || 10
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|ARU}} || 8 || 5 || 1 || 2 || 17 || 11
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|BAH}} || 3 || 3 || 0 || 0 || 11 || 5
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|BLZ}} || 2 || 0 || 1 || 1 || 0 || 1
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|BER}} || 16 || 5 || 4 || 7 || 20 || 29
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|BON}} || 2 || 2 || 0 || 0 || 7 || 3
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|BVI}} || 1 || 1 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 1
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|CAN}} || 3 || 0 || 0 || 3 || 2 || 9
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|CAY}} || 3 || 2 || 0 || 1 || 11 || 4
|- bgcolor="#ffffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|CRC}} || 2 || 1 || 0 || 1 || 2 || 4
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|CUB}} || 8 || 0 || 4 || 4 || 3 || 12
|- bgcolor="#ffffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|CUW}} || 2 || 1 || 0 || 1 || 2 || 4
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|DMA}} || 15 || 8 || 4 || 3 || 21 || 12
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|DOM}} || 5 || 0 || 1 || 4 || 3 || 14
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|SLV}} || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 3
|- bgcolor="#ffffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|FIN}} || 1 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 0 || 0
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|GUF}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 5
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|GRN}} || 28 || 11 || 12 || 5 || 48 || 32
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|GLP}} || 7 || 1 || 2 || 4 || 5 || 8
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|GUA}} || 3 || 0 || 1 || 2 || 1 || 5
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|GUY}} || 42 || 10 || 12 || 20 || 49 || 80
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|HAI}} || 4 || 1 || 0 || 3 || 6 || 11
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|JAM}} || 23 || 5 || 6 || 12 || 23 || 33
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|MTQ}} || 14 || 2 || 5 || 7 || 21 || 30
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|MSR}} || 3 || 1 || 0 || 2 || 9 || 7
|- bgcolor="#ffffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|ANT}} || 5 || 2 || 1 || 2 || 4 || 5
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|NCA}} || 3 || 0 || 0 || 3 || 1 || 10
|- bgcolor="#ffffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|NIR}} || 1 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 1 || 1
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|PAN}} || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 1
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|PUR}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 2
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|SKN}} || 12 || 4 || 2 || 6 || 19 || 21
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|LCA}} || 10 || 5 || 2 || 3 || 19 || 11
|- bgcolor="#ffffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|SMN}} || 2 || 1 || 0 || 1 || 4 || 1
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|VIN}} || 23 || 8 || 9 || 6 || 36 || 30
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|SUR}} || 12 || 4 || 5 || 3 || 13 || 10
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|SWE}} || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 4
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|TRI}} || 47 || 6 || 11 || 30 || 38 || 125
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|TCA}} || 2 || 2 || 0 || 0 || 7 || 0
|- bgcolor="#ccffcc"
| align="left" | {{fb|VIR}} || 7 || 6 || 0 || 1 || 20 || 1
|- bgcolor="#ffcccc"
| align="left" | {{fb|USA}} || 4 || 0 || 0 || 4 || 0 || 20
|-class="sortbottom"
! Total || 341 || 105 || 86 || 150 || 449 || 576
|}

==See also==
*[[Barbados national under-17 football team|Barbados men's national under-17 football team]]
*[[Football in Barbados]]
*[[Barbados 4–2 Grenada (1994 Caribbean Cup qualification)]]
*[[Sport in Barbados]]

==References==
{{reflist}}

==External links==
{{commons category}}
*{{official website}}
*[https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/BRB Barbados FIFA profile]

{{Barbados national football team}}
{{Football in Barbados}}
{{CONCACAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Barbados}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Barbados National Football Team}}
[[Category:Barbados national football team| ]]
[[Category:Caribbean national association football teams]]
[[Category:Football in Barbados]]
